hidden_environmental_cost: Requires plastic production from fossil fuels, transportation, and often becomes landfill or litter if not recycled.
key_hidden_impacts: PET plastic; fossil fuel use; single-use waste; ocean/landfill pollution
small_user_action: Carry a refillable bottle and use water fountains when possible.
data_note: EPA reported PET bottles and jars had a 29.1% recycling rate in 2018.

surface_description: Lightweight bag used for a few minutes.
hidden_environmental_cost: Plastic bags are made from fossil-fuel-based material and can become litter or long-lasting waste.
key_hidden_impacts: Short use life; litter risk; landfill waste; wildlife harm
small_user_action: Keep one reusable bag in your backpack or car.
data_note: University of Michigan notes that most U.S. plastic waste is landfilled and only a small share is recycled.
source_name: University of Michigan CSS Plastic Waste Factsheet

hidden_environmental_cost: Fast fashion can hide water use, textile waste, dye pollution, microplastic pollution, and carbon emissions.
key_hidden_impacts: Textile waste; water use; chemical dyes; microplastics; emissions
small_user_action: Buy fewer items and choose clothes you will wear many times.
data_note: UNEP reports the fashion/textiles sector contributes 2–8% of global greenhouse gas emissions and consumes large volumes of water.

hidden_environmental_cost: Single-use packaging creates waste and may be difficult to recycle if contaminated with food.
key_hidden_impacts: Single-use waste; contamination; landfill; plastic/foam pollution
small_user_action: Choose restaurants that use compostable or reusable packaging when possible.
data_note: EPA municipal waste data shows packaging is a major part of municipal solid waste.
source_name: US EPA Containers and Packaging: Product-Specific Data

surface_description: A convenient cup for coffee or tea.
hidden_environmental_cost: Many disposable cups include coatings or lids that make recycling harder and add single-use waste.
key_hidden_impacts: Paper/plastic mix; single-use cup; plastic lid; recycling difficulty
small_user_action: Bring a reusable cup when buying drinks.
data_note: EPA packaging data can support discussion of paper/plastic container waste.
source_name: US EPA Containers and Packaging: Product-Specific Data

surface_description: A normal fast-food or restaurant meal.
hidden_environmental_cost: Beef production can involve high greenhouse gas emissions, land use, water demand, and methane from cattle.
key_hidden_impacts: Methane emissions; land use; feed production; water use
small_user_action: Try replacing one beef meal per week with a lower-impact meal.
data_note: CarbonCloud benchmark reports beef hamburger footprint at store shelf; use as a general comparison, not exact local value.

surface_description: Food arrives quickly at your door.
hidden_environmental_cost: Delivery can add packaging waste and transportation emissions beyond the food itself.
key_hidden_impacts: Extra packaging; delivery transportation; single-use utensils; bags
small_user_action: Decline disposable utensils and combine orders when possible.
data_note: EPA food waste and packaging sources can support the hidden impact explanation.
source_name: US EPA Food Waste / Landfill Methane

surface_description: Food that seems small enough to throw away.
hidden_environmental_cost: Food waste in landfills produces methane as it decomposes without oxygen.
key_hidden_impacts: Methane; wasted water; wasted energy; landfill space
small_user_action: Save leftovers, plan portions, or compost food scraps.
data_note: EPA says food waste is about 24% of municipal solid waste disposed in landfills and landfills are a major methane source.
source_name: US EPA Quantifying Methane Emissions from Landfilled Food Waste

hidden_environmental_cost: Frequent upgrades can hide e-waste, mining impacts, manufacturing emissions, and difficult recycling.
key_hidden_impacts: E-waste; rare earth mining; battery materials; manufacturing emissions
eco_friendly_alternative: Keep phone longer or buy refurbished
small_user_action: Repair your phone or trade it in through certified recycling.
data_note: UN e-waste resources describe electronics waste as a growing waste stream with recycling challenges.

hidden_environmental_cost: Cardboard is often recyclable, but unnecessary shipping and packaging still use materials and transport energy.
key_hidden_impacts: Packaging material; delivery emissions; tape/plastic fillers; over-packaging
small_user_action: Reuse boxes and recycle clean cardboard.
data_note: EPA reports corrugated boxes had a high recycling rate in 2018, but they still represent a major packaging category.
source_name: US EPA Containers and Packaging: Product-Specific Data

hidden_environmental_cost: Aluminum is recyclable, but producing new aluminum can require significant energy and mining.
key_hidden_impacts: Mining; high energy production; packaging waste; transport
eco_friendly_alternative: Refillable drink container or recycled aluminum products
small_user_action: Recycle cans and avoid unnecessary single-use drinks.
data_note: EPA containers and packaging data supports recycling and waste context for aluminum containers.
source_name: US EPA Containers and Packaging: Product-Specific Data

surface_description: A fork, spoon, or knife used once.
hidden_environmental_cost: Small plastic items are often not recycled and can add to landfill or litter.
key_hidden_impacts: Single-use plastic; low recyclability; litter; fossil fuel material
small_user_action: Keep a reusable utensil set in your bag.
data_note: University of Michigan CSS gives broad context on low plastic recycling and high landfill share.
source_name: University of Michigan CSS Plastic Waste Factsheet

surface_description: A small receipt that seems harmless.
hidden_environmental_cost: Receipts add paper waste, and thermal paper can create recycling complications.
key_hidden_impacts: Paper use; thermal coating; waste; clutter
small_user_action: Choose email or no receipt when possible.
data_note: EPA paper and packaging data can support general paper waste context.
source_name: US EPA National Overview: Materials, Waste and Recycling

hidden_environmental_cost: Batteries contain materials that should be recycled properly and can create pollution risks if discarded incorrectly.
key_hidden_impacts: Metals; chemical leakage risk; e-waste; improper disposal
small_user_action: Use rechargeables and recycle batteries at approved drop-off sites.
data_note: EPA battery resources explain that batteries should be managed and recycled properly.

surface_description: A sweet drink in a plastic bottle.
hidden_environmental_cost: The drink includes packaging waste plus production and transportation impacts.
key_hidden_impacts: Plastic bottle; sugar production; transport; recycling limits
eco_friendly_alternative: Water from reusable bottle or fountain drink in reusable cup
data_note: EPA PET bottle recycling data supports plastic bottle impact context.
